entry.title: Monitor Material coverage App Insights - Shortage definitions
entry.content:
Intro:

Let us see some Information about the Monitor Material coverage app.

Overview.

Difference between MMC - Net segment vs Net / individual Segment - SAP Help & Blog 

Monitor Material Coverage - Net segment - Considers Planning Segments 20 and 22.

20 - An individual customer segment is created per individual customer order for make-to-order production.

22 - An individual project segment is created per project for project planning.

Monitor Material Coverage - Net / Individual segment - Considers Planning Segments 20, 22 and 24.

In addition to 20 and 22, '24 - Segment is created for materials that are directly produced or procured without planning' also considered.

In direct production/procurement the production/ Purchase orders will be created for components on creating the Production/purchase orders for header materials - special procurement key 52 - LINK

SIMG - Production - Material Requirements Planning - Planning - Direct Production/Procurement.

Prerequisite's

Define Material Shortage Profiles

SIMG - Production - Material Requirements Planning - Apps for Material Requirements Planning - General Settings - Define Material Shortage Profiles

Define Supply and Demand Profiles

Here you can define which receipts and requirements are to be considered for respective shortage definition profiles

SIMG - Production - Material Requirements Planning - Apps for Material Requirements Planning - General Settings - Define Supply and Demand Profiles

Main Part.

Master data - BOM - Materials PPA24, PPA22

Stock Availability bar - Used the Material PPA22

At beginning I have create a Master data and PIR demand. Later opened the MMC app. But I could not able to see any Stock Availability bar.

Later I change the chart setting to see the bar.

Colors in days field

In case you are facing the same issue pls change the time period in configure chart to cover the demand period.

Some times the color field is not working. Day supply field will show how many days will the available stock can fulfill the requirements. 

The color in respective fields are based on values maintained in the app Maintain Time-Dependent Stock Levels. The values are Safety days of supply, Target days of supply, Maximum days of supply.

Red color - No of First shortage days < SDOS - here 35 not < 10 so not applicable

Yellow color - SDOS =< No of first shortage days < TDOS - here 10= < 35 < 50 - applicable

Green color - SDOS < TDOS =< No of first shortage days - here 10 < 50 not =<  35 - NA

Click on stock availability and click check manage material coverage to see the requirements in details.

Definition of Fields in this app. Using the same below example 

First shortage on - Date on which the first shortage occurs.

Here its 24.10.2024 because I have a dependent requirement on that day.

Days' Supply - It will consider stock, receipt and requirements for calculating the material shortage days as like MD04. 

Days' supply = First shortage day - Current day = 24.10.2024 - 03.09.2024 = 35 days (Only working days)

You can use this link to calculate. Also took reference from Future evolution in MMC

Stock Day supply - It will consider only stocks and requirements for calculating material shortage days. Planned receipts are not considered. 

Stock Day supply = First shortage day (Excluding receipt) - Current day = 24.10.2024 - 03.09.2024 = 35 days

Intraday Supply = Days’ Supply + Fractional Coverage on the Day of the First Shortage.

Intraday Supply = Days' supply + Stock / Requirement on shortage day

                          =  35 + 23/30 = 35 + 0.36 = 35.76 days

Stock Intraday Supply = Stock Days’ Supply + Fractional Coverage on the Day of the First Shortage.

No receipts are Considered 

Stock Intraday Supply = Stock Days' supply + Stock / Requirement on shortage day

                          =  35 + 10 / 30 = 35 + 0.33 = 35.33 days

Shortage Definition - used the material PPB24 for this example (Link) 

Based on the prerequisites IMG configuration the system calculate the material shortage days. For example by considering the demands only or demands and supply or confirmed receipts or planned receipts. Here lets see result of the standard configurations.

MRP Standard - It will consider all supplies and demands to calculate the material shortage like MD04 as per the standard configs. (Made some changes)

Manage material coverage app

First Shortage day = 01.08.2024

Days' supply = First shortage day - Current day = 01.08.2024 - 03.09.2024 = -24 days

Stock Day supply = First shortage day (Excluding receipt) - Current day = 1 day (doubt)

Intraday Supply = Days' supply + Stock / Requirement on shortage day = -24 + 10/11 = -24.00 day (doubt)

Stock Intraday Supply = Stock Days' supply + Stock / Requirement on shortage day = 1 + 9/30 = 0.00 day (doubt)

Stock Day Supply - It will consider the stock and demands to calculate the material shortage as per the standard configs. No Receipts considered. PIR also not considered. You can add it by your own.

First Shortage day = 22.11.2024

Days' supply = First shortage day - Current day = 22.11.2024 - 03.09.2024 = 55 days

Stock Day supply = First shortage day (Excluding receipt) - Current day = 22.11.2024 - 03.09.2024 =  55 days

Intraday Supply = Days' supply + Stock / Requirement on shortage day = 55 + 9/30 = 55.30 days

Stock Intraday Supply = Stock Days' supply + Stock / Requirement on shortage day = 55 + 9/30 = 55.30 days

Ordered Requirements - It will consider the stock and receipts to calculate the material shortage as per standard configs. No demands considered

As there is no shortage every dates are 9999.

Ordered Receipts - It will considers the stock, demand and confirmed receipts to calculate the material shortage as per the standard configs. No Purchase Requisition or Planned order considered

First Shortage day = 22.11.2024

Days' supply = First shortage day - Current day = 22.11.2024 - 03.09.2024 = 55 days

Stock Day supply = First shortage day (Excluding receipt) - Current day = 22.11.2024 - 03.09.2024 =  55 days

Intraday Supply = Days' supply + Stock / Requirement on shortage day = 55 + 11/30 = 55.36 days (doubt)

Stock Intraday Supply = Stock Days' supply + Stock / Requirement on shortage day = 55 + 9/30 = 55.30 days 

Note: I have expected that the Days' Supply, Stock Day supply, Intraday Supply, Stock Intraday Supply will be calculated according to the shortage definition in initial screen of MMC app. Its working inside the manage material coverage app in real time, but not in Monitor material coverage app.

I have increased the evaluation horizon of other shortage definition profiles.

After some times these Days' Supply, Stock Day supply, Intraday Supply, Stock Intraday Supply days were calculated respectively based on the shortage definition. Kindly share your experience if you faced any delay in this app.

Conclusion:

I hope you this may help you on understanding the Monitor Material coverage app. Please refer Link & this to have good idea on implementing the changes in it. Please clarify on (doubt) areas

entry.title: Re: Internal Error (-1) Occured [131-183] during Quick Co...
entry.content:
hi @francesca3007 

(I apologize in advance for my spelling mistakes. I use translate because my English is not good.)

I encountered a similar problem today. Since I could not find a clear solution from what was written, I wanted to share what I found. First of all, the link I used is here;
https://www.sapxiaocui.top/index.php/archives/33/

My problem: When I ran the quick copy, I was getting the error "Internal error(-1) 131-183".
I found my faulty tables using the queries that show errors in user-defined tables and fields from the link I gave above.

 

Spoiler
DETECTION QUERIES FOR HANA:
1) UDT is defined but does not exist. 

select T0."TableName" from OUTB T0 where not exists (select 1 from sys.tables T1 where T1.schema_name = current_schema and T1.is_system_table = 'FALSE' and T1.table_name = '@' || "TableName");

this query gave me inconsistent tables that were not in Hana Sql but were in Sap Business One user tables.
Since these tables were deleted, I could not delete them from user defined tables. For this, I recreated the relevant tables and deleted them from Sap user tables.
When I tried again, the connection was established successfully and I performed the copy operation.

 

I hope it helps others.
Good work.

entry.title: Re: Single Sign On
entry.content:
Hi Janet,

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, including its CBC, has its own Cloud Identity Services to control access. As far as how to configure SSO, you can refer to https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services/cloud-identity-services/auth-idp-enable-idp-initiated-sso-from-all-corporate-identity-providers and other related documentation. 

By the way, CBC is for customer business scenario configurations, not for authorization related work.

Regards,

George

entry.title: Building a business data fabric: SAP Datasphere vs. DIY implementations
entry.content:
Providing your teams with relevant, real-time analysis enables them to make more informed and agile business decisions. But we often hear from customers that scattered information and siloed data prevent their employees from getting the rapid insights they need.

That’s why so many enterprises are looking to revolutionize data management by building a business data fabric. This is a data management architecture that focuses on delivering an integrated, semantically-rich data layer over underlying data landscapes to provide seamless and scalable access to data without duplication. But when it comes to building a business data fabric, what is the best approach?

Putting SAP Datasphere to the test

SAP Datasphere, a comprehensive data service built on SAP Business Technology Platform, is the foundation for a business data fabric. It enables organizations to deliver meaningful data to every data consumer—with business context and logic intact. It provides capabilities from data ingestion through self-service data access across SAP and non-SAP data.  

In its Data Fabric Field Test: SAP vs. DIY, GigaOm measured the ease of use, time, effort, and total cost of deploying SAP Datasphere as compared to a do-it-yourself (DIY) approach to building a data fabric with a blend of other applications. The DIY implementation path included solutions for data warehousing and data integration, along with a data catalog and data lake. The results are very compelling:  

Ease of use

GigaOm evaluated the ease of use when it comes to carrying out a variety of data management tasks. It found that, compared to the DIY approach, SAP Datasphere was on average 23.1% easier to use, with data modeling 72.4% easier and data security 78.6% easier.

Time

The report found that deploying SAP Datasphere took significantly less time than building the DIY implementation path. In fact, the initial build timeline for SAP Datasphere was about 2.5 times shorter.

Effort

To quantify effort, GigaOm evaluated the amount of effort required for enterprises to complete the three main stages of establishing a data fabric, which they defined as:

  1. Initial build/migration
  2. Continuous integration and delivery (CI/CD) of new features and enhancements
  3. Administration (including maintenance and user support)

For all three stages, the report found that deploying SAP Datasphere requires less effort than the DIY approach. And for the CI/CD and maintenance stages, GigaOm found that it takes about a third to a half of the headcount to run SAP Datasphere as compared to the DIY implementation path.

Total cost

Across all data fabric sizes, the infrastructure costs to support SAP Datasphere were about 69% lower than those of the DIY approach. The report also found that SAP Datasphere provided a savings of 63% on initial build costs, 53% on CI/CD costs, and 41% on administration costs. What’s more, the solution reduced total cost of ownership by 138% over a three-year period.


Find out more

In my next two blogs, I’ll highlight the ROI and deployment best practices for SAP Datasphere. In the meantime, read the GigaOm Data Fabric Field Test: SAP vs. DIY study to learn more.

entry.title: Re: Safety Technician Persona in SAP Service and Asset Manager
entry.content: The new Fiori-based WCM in SAP S/4HANA is called Permit To Work (PTW). This was built on the new version of PM supporting a phased approach. SSAM is completely integrated with this too, however it's important to mention LOTO is missing from PTW, therefore the safety technician persona has less features in this case (everything like with the legacy WCM, except for the LOTO).

entry.title: Re: Debug Tips for program RFFOEDI1
entry.content:
Hello,
Excellent explanation. Very useful...

It is difficult to send a pdf file that generates the payment advice through F110 to the SAP Ariba portal.

Ariba, indicates that you implement an extension,

https://help.sap.com/docs/sisgw/sap-ariba-cloud-integration-gateway-migration-guide/how-to-implement-customer-exit-for-payment-remittance-to-fill-data-into-custom-segments?locale=en-US&version=2405

This is already implemented, but a segment must be created in that include (ZXF08U01) E1ARBCIG_ATTACH_HDR_DET. This segment is part of the idoc that goes to the SAP Ariba portal. And it must be filled with the pdf in hex format. (OTF)

In the program RFFOEDI1 that is used by F110. It makes a call on line 367, it calls the FI_EDI_REMADV_PEXR2001_OUT function, and reaches the exit ZXF08U01. At this point it is not possible to obtain the OTF that is required..

It is difficult to obtain the OTF in the ZXF08U01 include, and be able to send it to the SAP Ariba portal.

There is a similar topic but for POs. But it does not work for payments in F110, I leave a link

https://community.sap.com/t5/spend-management-blogs-by-members/send-sapscript-form-pdf-from-output-type-message-to-business-network/ba-p/13579642

I appreciate your understanding.

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Read/validate xml namespace using groovy script in SA...
entry.content:
@Ryan-Crosby - so sorry to bother you again but facing a weird issue with xsl when I test with payload with body in it.

This is ultimately causing the iflow to fail as below statement has an error

 <xsl:value-of select="sapci:setProperty($exchange, 'MyProperty', $nspace)"/>

XSL:

<xsl:stylesheet version="3.0" xmlns:xsl="http://www.w3.org/1999/XSL/Transform" xmlns:sapci="http://sap.com/it/" exclude-result-prefixes="sapci">
<xsl:param name="exchange"/>
<xsl:template match="/">
<xsl:variable name="nspace" select="//namespace::*"/>
<message>
<xsl:value-of select="$nspace"/>
</message>
</xsl:template>
</xsl:stylesheet>

Example Payload with <Field1> in the body:

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<root xmlns="http://example.com/a" xmlns:ns2="http://example.com/b"> 
  <Field1>Hello</Field1>
 </root>          

XSL output produces multiple lines

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<message>
http://example.com/a http://example.com/b http://www.w3.org/XML/1998/namespace
http://example.com/a http://example.com/b http://www.w3.org/XML/1998/namespace
</message>

But if I remove the <Field1> in the payload, xml output is 

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?><message>http://example.com/a http://example.com/b http://www.w3.org/XML/1998/namespace</message>

entry.title: Re: How to influence pptx filename on SEN playback?
entry.content:
Hi Wallace,

Sorry, I don't think that there is any way to modify the name of the uploaded PPT file inside Producer.  Producer renames the content object so that it can uniquely manage that object and prevents the unique ID from being changed.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: How to automatically bulk create virtual tables dynamically in code?
entry.content: Hi we are accessing many tables and views from a remote system and wondered how to bulk create virtual tables?

entry.title: Re: Read/validate xml namespace using groovy script in SA...
entry.content: I will take a look at it tomorrow when I'm sitting down in my office.

entry.title: Re: How to automatically bulk create virtual tables dynam...
entry.content:
Hi this code will create bulk VT to the remote system.

first manually create virtual table locally to the SYS.table_columns system catalog in the remote. Assuming someone in admin has set up a remote source in your local system and you have access to system objects. 

eg in this example we are using table_columns to source table names.

CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE "MY_LOCAL_SCHEMA"."vt_table_columns_remote_db"
AT "MY_Remote_source_name"."MYDb"."SYS"."TABLE_COLUMNS";

This below select will generate the SQL code for all the VT create statements you need.

SELECT DISTINCT 
'CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE ' || '"' || schema_name || '"."VT_' || table_name || 
'" AT ' || '"MY_Remote_source_name"."MYDb".' || 
'"' || schema_name || '"."' || table_name || '";'
FROM 
(
SELECT DISTINCT 
schema_name,
table_name
FROM vt_table_columns_remote_db
)
Just cut and paste this code and run. Note, it is not possible (currently) to exec immediate the code generated from this SQL you have to cut and paste and run the output. FYI.
 
You can also create VT to views by using the SYS.views similarly.
 
You can add filters as required. 
WHERE  table_name LIKE '%MYTABLES%'
AND schema_name LIKE '%MYSCHEMAS%'
 
Note any tables with geometry are not supported so you can ad this filter.
AND table_name NOT IN
(SELECT DISTINCT table_name
FROM vt_table_columns_remote_db
WHERE DATA_TYPE_NAME LIKE 'ST%'
 
To work around this issue see my post here

entry.title: Regarding the Automated Operation of GUIMenuBar
entry.content:
When using "VB. Net" to execute the following code, statements that operate on GUIMenuBar are invalid，
session.findById("wnd[0]/mbar/menu[0]/menu[3]/menu[1]").select()

The complete code is as follows：

Dim SapGuiAuto As Object = Nothing
Dim application As Object = Nothing
Dim connection As Object = Nothing
Dim session As Object = Nothing

Try
SapGuiAuto = GetObject("SAPGUI")
application = SapGuiAuto.GetScriptingEngine


如果不是 TypeOf 应用程序是 GuiApplication，则
SapGuiAuto = Nothing
返回
End 如果

connection = 应用程序。儿童（0）

如果不是 TypeOf 连接是 GuiConnection，则
应用程序 = 无
SapGuiAuto = 无
返回
End 如果

session = 连接。儿童（0）

如果不是 TypeOf 会话是 GuiSession 则
连接 = 无
应用程序 = 无
SapGuiAuto = 无
返回
结束 If
session.findById（“wnd[0]/tbar[0]/okcd”）。文本 = “/nZ9PP22”
session.findById（“wnd[0]”）.sendVKey（0）
session.findById（“wnd[0]/usr/ctxt[0]”）.文本 = “N”
session.findById（“wnd[0]/usr/ctxt[1]”）。文本 = “334921201”
session.findById（“wnd[0]/usr/ctxt[3]”）。文本 = “2701”
session.findById（“wnd[0]/usr/rad[2]”）.选择
session.findById（“wnd[0]/tbar[1]/btn[8]”）.press

session.findById（“wnd[0]/usr/cntlGRID1/shellcont/shell”）.setCurrentCell（-1， “PLPOD-VORNR”）
session.findById（“wnd[0]/usr/cntlGRID1/shellcont/shell”）.selectColumn（“PLPOD-VORNR”）

session.findById（“wnd[0]/tbar[1]/btn[29]”）.press
session.findById（“wnd[1]/usr/ssub/1/ctxt[0]”）.Text = “0020”
session.findById（“wnd[1]/tbar[0]/btn[0]”）.press's.findById
（“wnd[0]/tbar[1]/btn[45]”）.press
Dim savepath As String = $“C：\Users\{APS_Auxiliary_Form.id（）}\Downloads\”
Dim filename As String = “z9ppp22_23_order.xlsx”
Dim _filepath As String = Path.Combine（savepath， filename）
session.findById（“wnd[0]/mbar/menu[0]/menu[3]/menu[1]”）.select（）
Catch ex As Exception
Console.WriteLine（例如消息）
Finally
session = 无
连接 = 无应用程序
= 无
SapGuiAuto = 无

entry.title: New bank Transfer payment method setting
entry.content:
Hello,

I made a payment data and clearing document.  

I have some questions.

1) Does it connect with MBC?

2) How is connect the bank information and transfer real payment?

3) Is it can use the workflows of paying bank account?

Thank you in advance.

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: New bank Transfer payment method setting
entry.content:
Hello,

For MBC, if you have activated the scope item 16R, then the system will automatically send payment instructions, receive status notifications, receive bank statements, and receive lockbox messages. And you can find the test script of the scope item via https://me.sap.com/processnavigator/SolP/16R.

For setting workflows of paying bank account, you can reach for test script of scope item J78 where the URL is https://me.sap.com/processnavigator/SolS/EARL_SolS-013/2408/SolP/J78?region=DE.

Also here is some documents may help you Payments and Bank Communications | SAP Help Portal.

Please make sure if you have activated the related scope item when using the specific functions.

entry.title: Re: Facing 401 unauthorized error while trying post data to CPI via SAP API Management
entry.content:
Hi Madhura,

HTTP 404 is fine when doing a connectivity test. It simply is a ping test to the Cloud Integration Tenant and means its gone through successfully. 
HTTP 401 comes when you have an unauthorised access. This might be coming when you are hitting the exact iflow URL via APIM. 
Please check the username and password that was configured in keyvalue map. Another way to check that your credential is being passed correctly is to check the request headers just before hitting the Cloud Integration Tenant and ensure that the Authorization header has the exact same value as you would have in Postman. Base 64 encrypted username/password.

Please refer to the below two posts and also have a read through the comments and these might be helpful.

entry.title: 在销售领域充分利用机器学习能力：使用 SAP 分析云实现高级价格批量预测
entry.content:
简介 

不断变化的销售环境给企业带来了许多挑战，企业不仅需要准确地计划其销售量和收入预测，还要考虑价格变化对这些预测的影响。本博文将介绍企业如何利用 SAP 分析云以及其许可中包含的“面向公司 FP&A 的内容”快速销售计划包，优化销售量价格计划。 

 

除了快速软件包中的计划选项外，我们还内置了报表和分析功能，以便用户更好地了解价格-数量组合，并将结果合并入计划中。如果您感兴趣，我很乐意在未来的博客中讨论这些选项。   

现在我们将主要介绍：基于机器学习的预测场景的使用，以及在预测中将价格作为重要影响因素进行集成。 

以下示例中，我将介绍如何简单地调整 SAP 分析云中的现有内容，以享受到机器学习预测带来的好处。 

使用 SAP 分析云进行销售量价格计划 

SAP 分析云为公司提供了一个用于分析和计划销售活动的强大平台。通过免费的“公司 FP&A 内容”快速销售计划包，公司可以使用现成的仪表盘和模型，来快速、准确地创建销售预测，同时考虑价格的波动变化。在标准包中，计划者可以非常灵活地计划数量、价格、折扣和其他变量。为了引入机器学习生成的预测结果，我想扩展 SAP 分析云的功能包中的一些选项。这会减少计划中需要的手动工作量，因为大部分预测都可以从自动预测中生成。 

预测场景和机器学习 

销售量价格计划的一个重要方面是使用机器学习支持的预测场景。这些预测场景分析历史销售数据和其他相关因素，以自动生成考虑价格变化的未来销售预测。价格更改是计划员输入的未来变量，作为预测期间内机器预测的影响因素。 

将价格集成为影响因素 

销售量价格计划的一个重要组成部分是，将价格视为销售预测的影响因素。通过将价格数据集成到分析中，公司可以更好地了解价格变化将如何影响其未来的销售数据。这些信息使公司能够对其定价策略做出明智的决策，并相应地调整其销售预测。同时，由于系统自动将价格作为预测的影响因素，因此也可以显著减少手动工作量。 

对未来的展望 

为了展示价格变化对未来销售数据的影响，在示例公司中，我们计划将自行车（“山地自行车”）的极端价格设置在三月（示例中提供了截至2023年9月的实际数据）。在预测中添加这种极端的价格后，我们可以看看这种变化将如何影响销售数据以及整体的销售目标。 

SAP 分析云中场景的结构 

我们需要以下版本（场景）： 

  • 实际（截至 2023 年 9 月的实际数据） 
  • 计划（计划场景，其中还将计划未来价格） 
  • Predictive_ML（传输实际数据和未来价格的场景）。 

“拟合预测方案”是在 Predictive_ML 版本的私有副本上执行的。预测运行后，预测值将传输到私有版本，也可以复制回到 Predictive_ML 版本。这些操作（数据操作）的执行由嵌入 SAP 分析云故事中的按钮触发。 

结论： 

销售量价格计划是许多公司销售成功的关键之处。通过使用 SAP 分析云和预测场景，公司可以改进销售预测并制定有关销售策略的更明智的决策。通过集成价格作为影响因素，公司可以更好地了解价格变化对其销售数据的影响，并相应地调整其策略。通过清晰地了解未来和减少手动工作量，公司可以更有效地实现销售目标，并确保销售的长期成功。SAP 分析云和内容包（例如快速销售计划和分析）可帮助解决此问题。 

希望本博客给您提供了足够的信息和启发。如果您有任何问题、反馈或想法要分享，欢迎随时与我们联系。您的意见对于未来的讨论和内容很有价值。感谢您的阅读，希望您在使用 SAP 分析云进行销售计划工作时顺利。 

 

原文作者：dirkpelz 

原文链接：Unleashing AI and Machine Learning in Sales: Advanced Price-Volume Forecasting with SAP Analytics Cloud

entry.title: Re: multi-currency pasting -invalid currency
entry.content:
Hi,

The error 'Invalid Currency' appears if:

1. The user enters the value in Debit(FC) and/or Credit(FC) field and do not mention the Currency Code for a Multi Currency G/L Account or BP. The system for a multi currency G/L Account or BP cannot be itself identify which currency user wants to post the entry for.

2. The error can also appear, if the Currency Code taken is not correct. So, for example, if you want to enter USD 100 and instead mention USF 100, the error will appear. The USF is not a currency code defined in the Currencies setup.

Kr,

Jitin

entry.title: Re: solman_setup - Database hosts when connecting a manag...
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

You are correct that in order to view the DB servers in Managed System Configuration, the LMDB must have complete information about the DB, including the "Technical Instances" and "Hosts," unless it is a virtual type of DB. In certain cases, like ORACLE RAC, HANA SR, HANA MDC, etc., there are specific associations that control the behavior, such as SAP_IdenticalDatabaseSystem and SAP_DatabaseReplicationDependency and so on. 

Useful KBA to send DB to SOLMAN: 3232202 - Guided Answer for Troubleshooting for missing or outdated LMDB data suppliers in SAP Solution Manager and SAP Focused Run.

Best regards,
Vera Jiang

entry.title: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – Upgrade Faster – Major shifts in SAP Fiori implementation
entry.content:
Upgrading your SAP S/4HANA is often a rare opportunity to shift your user experience fundamentals.  Knowing which shifts to make with your technical upgrade or post-upgrade simplifies planning effort and resources. 

Image of a forklift moving a container - Image source: SAP 

In this post are some practical tips on timing when to shift assuming:

  • your source system is a SAP S/4HANA 2022 or lower, and
  • your target SAP S/4HANA system is SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01 or higher

These are the major shifts in SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA user experience as at SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01 or higher:

  • Move from standalone (hub) FES to embedded FES
  • Move custom technical catalogs from Launchpad Designer to Launchpad App Manager
  • Move launchpad layouts from groups to spaces and pages
  • Move to SAP Build Work Zone, including the optional move to SAP Mobile Start.

Hint: This blog post is relevant for customers running SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition.  For example: customers running RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, or SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition tailored option. This blog post is also relevant for customers running the SAP S/4HANA product on other infrastructure such as on-premise server or hyperscaler.

 

Moving from standalone (hub) FES to embedded (FES)

Important: You only need to do this move if you are NOT already using embedded FES. Most RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition will already be using embedded FES. If you are not sure, check with your technical team.

A very simplified diagram below shows the difference. Essentially:

  • With standalone FES your SAP Fiori front-end server is a separate server to your SAP S/4HANA server
  • With embedded FES your SAP Fiori front-end server is a software component of your SAP S/4HANA server

 

RECOMMENDATION: Do WITH your technical upgrade

 

Why move to embedded FES as part of technical upgrade 

While the benefits are considerable, this is still a disruptive architectural shift. You will need to:

  • Add new components to your SAP S/4HANA server
  • Adjust sizing on your SAP S/4HANA server for the additional usage
  • Adjust configuration on your SAP S/4HANA server and Web Dispatcher
  • You may need to adjust some integrations, for example move standalone apps to SAP Build Work Zone
  • Re-deploy any custom-built apps to your SAP S/4HANA server or to SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP)
  • Rework business-as-usual support procedures for security authorizations transports, maintenance, and troubleshooting
  • Retire your existing SAP Fiori front-end server

Embedded FES is planned to be the only option for SAP Fiori front-end server deployment for SAP S/4HANA from SAP S/4HANA 2025.

Refer to:

  • Blog - SAP Fiori deployment options and SAP Fiori front-end server strategy - UPDATE 2023
  • SAP Fiori Deployment Options and System Landscape Recommendations <Fiori landscape and deployment)

Embedded FES reduces system landscape complexity:

  • By installing the SAP Fiori front-end server (FES) directly on the (backend) SAP S/4HANA server
  • By applying strict release relationships between FES and backend server

Embedded FES typically reduces architecture costs:

  • Due to a reduced number of servers in the landscape
  • You also reduce associated operational costs, such as patching, managing transports, and managing security authorizations.
  • You should note that the backend SAP S/4HANA server may need to be resized to cover the additional frontend usage. Refer to SAP Quick Sizer tool and guidelines.

Refer to:

  • SAP Sizing – Quick Sizer guide
  • SAP Help - Quick Sizer Tool
  • SAP Help – SAP Fiori Overview – Plan your SAP Fiori implementation project
  • SAP Note 2467172 - How to size Fiori applications based on number of users

Embedded FES provides agility-at-scale by providing rapid activation task lists and other improved operational tooling. that reduce effort and speed up managing your SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA content. For example,

  • Task lists for setting up the Fiori launchpad, activating/updating SAP and custom content, updating security authorizations for custom business roles
  • Tools for managing content at scale, including launchpad app manager, launchpad content manager, manage launchpad spaces and manage launchpad pages
  • Simplified configuration, for launchpad features, for the user help provided by SAP Companion (and optionally SAP Enable Now)
  • Improved diagnostics and performance monitoring, via tools such as App Support, and STATS_FE

Customer experiences suggest that this shift is easiest to manage as part of a technical upgrade. Expect:

  • major impacts on your support team, particularly basis administrators and security authorization administrators
  • minor to moderate impacts on your business users, such as the loss of some personalization

 

How to move to embedded FES 

Option 1: Do a formal transition from standalone (hub) FES to embedded FES.

Refer to:

  • SAP Help – SAP Fiori Overview – Update, Upgrade, and Migrate
  • Blog - SAP Fiori transition from Standalone to Embedded Deployment in SAP S/4HANA
  • Moving from Standalone to Embedded Deployment in SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA

Option 2: Consider setting up embedded FES from scratch.

This can be a good option that saves time, especially if you currently use less than 100 SAP Fiori apps, including custom-built apps based on SAP Fiori UX, such as SAP Fiori design, SAP Fiori elements, and/or SAPUI5 development.

You have the advantage of using new rapid activation tools immediately to set up your embedded FES to current best practices.

To migrate your current content, consider that:

  • Custom-built apps can be exported/imported to change where they are stored (i.e. to move from one SAPUI5 ABAP Repository to another, or using SAP Fiori Tools to re-deploy the app to SAP BTP)
  • Fiori launchpad personalization can be exported/imported.
  • There are some restrictions on moving app views (aka app variants) – read the Additional considerations at the end of this section.

Refer to:

  • SAPUI5 SDK - Using the SAPUI5 ABAP Repository Upload and Download Reports to Synchronize
  • SAP Help – SAP Fiori Tools – Deploying an application
  • SAP Help – SAP Fiori launchpad administration guide – section Migrate Personalization Data
  • SAP Note 2789848 - Migration report for homepage and application personalization

Option 3: You can use a Hybrid approach that includes a move to SAP Build Work Zone alongside option 1 or 2.

This can be useful if you have a large number of custom-built apps, 3rd party apps, standalone (non-S/4HANA) apps), or SAP Cloud solutions that you want to manage side-by-side on SAP Build Work Zone.

Important: SAP Build Work Zone is NOT a replacement of embedded FES for SAP S/4HANA. Your SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA content must still be deployed on your SAP S/4HANA server and federated to SAP Build Work Zone via content exposure from SAP S/4HANA.

  • Refer to: Blog - SAP Fiori deployment options and SAP Fiori front-end server strategy - UPDATE 2023
  • SAP Fiori Deployment Options and System Landscape Recommendations <Fiori landscape and deployment)
  • SAP Help – SAP Build Work Zone - Federation of Remote Content Providers
  • SAP Help – SAP Fiori launchpad administration guide – Exposing launchpad content to SAP BTP

 

Additional considerations for moving to embedded FES

Considerations for your support team

Make sure your team is skilled up in the latest recommended tools and processes. In most cases, moving to the embedded server provides many opportunities to simplify and speed up business-as-usual support. For example, by using rapid content activation for SAP Business Roles and custom business roles.

Considerations for your business users

Set an expectation that some user personalization is likely to be lost.  There is some migration tooling for personalization however customer experiences so far suggest that almost always you lose some personalization. The degree of loss depends on the number of apps used, personalization features used, and the difference between your source and target SAP S/4HANA releases. 

Be aware that there some restrictions on moving app views (aka app variants), partly due to lifecycle shifts in how public views are managed. You may want to take the opportunity to downgrade public views to private views where needed. You can explicitly recreate new public views using Adapt UI. Refer to:

  • SAP Note 3019518 - Report to convert customer variants to private variants
  • Blog - SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – Yes Key Users can create default app settings for filters, tables, and charts

How to evaluate your current app usage

SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition and on-premise customers can find some statistics on your current app usage through SAP Early Watch. Refer to:

  • SAP EarlyWatch Alert Dashboard - New statistics about "Used SAPUI5 Applications"

 

Technical Catalog Migration – moving technical catalogs from Launchpad Designer to Launchpad App Manager

Important: You only need to do this move if you have created custom technical catalogs using the Launchpad Designer on SAP S/4HANA 2022 or lower releases and are now upgrading to SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01 or higher.

Customers starting on SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01 or higher are recommended to use Launchpad app manager only.  You should avoid using Launchpad Designer.

 

RECOMMENDATION: Do WITH your technical upgrade

 

Why migrate technical catalogs with your technical upgrade

This is a minor technical shift which is quick to complete. Typical expected effort is a few hours to a few days, depending on the number of custom catalogs created.

Moving to the launchpad app manager makes business-as-usual support and maintenance of launchpad content easier. That is, the launchpad app manager provides features that support agility at scale, such as:

  • a strict alignment of tiles and target mappings
  • adaptation features to maximize reuse of SAP technical catalogs

There is typically no impact on business users, so it is difficult to justify the activity as a separate functional project.

 

How to migrate technical catalogs

Run the Technical Catalog Migration tool across all of your custom technical catalogs.

Refer to:

  • SAP Note 3444719 - Documentation Correction: Missing Topics in SAP Fiori Launchpad Guide (SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01)
  • SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA - Technical Catalog Migration – Why and Getting Ready for Migration
  • SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA - Technical Catalog Migration – How the migration process works

 

Additional considerations for technical catalog migration

There can be extra effort involved if you do not have a clear separation between your cross-client custom technical catalogs and your client-specific custom business catalogs. For example, if you have custom technical catalogs assigned directly to business roles, or if you have separated tiles and target mappings into different catalogs.

The migration tool will help identify catalogs that need to be adjusted prior to migration.

 

Interim measures on your source SAP S/4HANA release to minimize migration effort on upgrade

  • If you have a single-level catalog design, start to split out into technical catalogs (cross-client) and business catalogs (client-specific)
  • Only create new custom technical catalogs in Launchpad App Manager
  • Avoid rework by locking as many people as possible out of Launchpad Designer

 

Moving from Groups to Spaces and Pages

Important: You only need to do this move if you are currently using Groups layout mode for your launchpad and are now upgrading to SAP S/4HANA 2021 or higher. 

Customers starting on SAP S/4HANA 2021 or higher are recommended to use spaces and pages only.

Be aware that functionality and features will depend on your SAP S/4HANA release. For example, new My Home page is available from SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01.

 

RECOMMENDATION: Do Post-Upgrade

 

Image: Summary of Groups versus Spaces and pages with My Home - key differences and benefits as at SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01. Source: SAP.

 

Why move to spaces and pages post-upgrade

There are significant, mostly positive, impacts on business users:

  • Provide lean, consistent, hierarchical pages that reflect your work responsibilities
  • More tile size options to reduce whitespace
  • Improved personalization options, such as space personalization to hide/show and sort space tabs
  • User personalizations automatically merged with administrator changes, supporting process changes over time
  • Option for merged spaces or pages for complex business roles
  • Improved performance, as only the current page is loaded at runtime

Image: Key benefits for spaces and pages for the business user.  Image source: SAP.

You have better process control by using spaces and pages to reflect desire work practices:

  • Reduced training for new recruits, because spaces and pages reflect job responsibilities
  • Reduced re-training for existing staff after process changes, because administrator changes to spaces and pages reflect process changes over item
  • For example, some customers use spaces, pages, and tiles to reinforce their BPMN process models. When processes change, layouts can be adjusted to reinforce the process change. This simplifies change management activities.

Hint: From SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01, spaces and pages include the very attractive new Home page. In other words, it is an obvious disadvantage to be using Groups layout from SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01.

Image: Spaces and pages in a nutshell - key features and behaviours. Image source: SAP.

 

There are significant, mostly positive, operational impacts on administering security authorizations:

  • Spaces (and their assigned pages) can be reused across multiple roles, including composite roles
  • WYSIWYG editors and role-context preview checks to support role alignment
  • Administrator changes automatically merged with user personalizations, supporting process changes over time
  • Read-only report Overview of Roles, Spaces, and Pages that can be shared with business stakeholders and auditors
  • Where-used support in maintenance tools such as launchpad content manager

Image: Key benefits of spaces and pages for administrators. Image source: SAP.

Step-wise user-by-user or role-by-role move is supported. This is definitely worth discussing with your business sponsors and stakeholders:

  • Stages are controlled by launchpad configuration parameters
  • You can use the “Enable Spaces” setting option to permit users to toggle between groups and spaces for piloting purposes
  • To speed up your move, you can use SAP delivered spaces and pages as a starting point; create your own custom spaces and pages; or convert current groups to spaces and pages via the migration tool.

 

How to move to spaces and pages post-upgrade

You should consider how you will govern your spaces and pages design. This maximizes business effectiveness, minimizes rework; minimizes maintenance effort, and reduces training for business users. 

For example,

  • Consider who should be responsible/accountable/consulted/informed for administrator-controlled space and page content. For example, you may want your business process experts to take ownership of the page design.
  • Consider how spaces and pages will be updated as process changes are introduced
  • Consider how spaces and pages will be updated when you upgrade to future releases

You should discuss with your current business stakeholders:

  • Whether current groups are meeting business needs, i.e. are they worth the migration effort
  • Whether SAP delivered spaces and pages for equivalent business roles are an effective starting point for business needs
  • Whether a fully custom design is needed, and how to minimize the effort of this approach
  • Discuss whether the business should take ownership of page layouts. You have the option to grow this ownership over time. For example:
    • Initial review and sign-off
    • Proposing changes to layouts and testing them
    • Making changes to layouts as a key user (technical team would still have responsibility for transports)

For any custom-built spaces and pages, you should use a templated approach to the id, title, and content of spaces and pages.

For example:

  • Consider using space and page templates, where available for your release
  • Consider naming conventions for space/page ids for the administrator
  • Consider naming conventions for space/page titles for users
  • SAP delivered spaces typically include an Overview page for daily tasks
  • SAP delivered spaces typically include one or more pages named for regular business processes, e.g. Purchase Requisition Processing
  • SAP delivered spaces may also include pages named for seasonal or exception processes, e.g. Tax Declaration

 

Additional considerations for moving to spaces and pages

You should consider moving users step-wise to spread the migration effort over time.  You can use the launchpad configuration parameters to control default features. For example:

  • Leave your current groups as-is. There is no need to delete them. 
  • Technically activate the spaces features with your technical upgrade or immediately after upgrade, but leave “groups” as the default for all users
  • Move first users who will be assigned urgent new innovations.
    • For example, you can Enable Spaces settings option to enable users to toggle between groups and spaces while they get used to the new layouts
  • Defer moving users whose current roles have no or minimal changes
    • Consider a simple templated approach for these users
  • Once all users are moved make spaces the default.

Refer to:

  • Blog - Getting Started with Spaces and Pages
  • Blog - Merging Spaces and Pages
  • Blog - Info Package: Making the Move from Launchpad Groups to Spaces (In SAP S/4HANA and in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition)

Interim measures on your source SAP S/4HANA release to minimize effort on upgrade

You can start moving role-by-role earlier, if you are on SAP S/4HANA 2021 or higher.

You can technically activate spaces and pages in your current release, but leave groups as the default. This enables you to start to exploring the main features and review differences in your target SAP S/4HANA release.

Hint: From SAP S/4HANA 2020 it is technically possible to use spaces and pages.  However for most customers is likely to be too early as there are no SAP-delivered spaces and pages for business roles as a starting point, and only basic features available.

 

Moving to SAP Build Work Zone and SAP Mobile Start

 

RECOMMENDATION: This move is optional and can be done any time.  You may want to defer this shift to post-upgrade.

 

Image: Example screenshots of the entry page to SAP Build Work Zone and SAP Mobile Start. Image source: SAP.

 

Why move to SAP Build Work Zone post-upgrade

SAP Build Work Zone provides an additional layer to:

  • Provide internet access to selected roles
  • Provide mobile access to selected roles
  • Provide external partner access to selected roles
  • Provide access across multiple cloud and on-premise solutions

Refer to: SAP Help - SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition

You may need to consider commercial aspects, such as the use of your RISE BTP credits.

You will need to consider architecture and cyber security aspects.

You will need to consider current restrictions, such as which content can be exposed, and which content can be federated from SAP S/4HANA. Refer to: SAP Help - SAP Build Work Zone - Restrictions

  • Be aware that these restrictions change frequently so review the roadmap when making plans.
  • You need to review content exposure options and implications. For example, using Spaces and Pages versus Spaces and Pages – New Experience

Refer to: SAP Help - SAP Fiori launchpad - Exposing Launchpad Content to SAP BTP

 

How to move to SAP Build Work Zone

You will need to configure the SAP Build Work Zone site to support your use cases.

You will need to federate the content from SAP S/4HANA using the content exposure. Refer to: SAP Help - SAP Build Work Zone - Integrating Business Content and SAP Help - SAP Fiori launchpad - Exposing Launchpad Content to SAP BTP

You will need to test your configuration from multiple entry points including:

  • Different web browsers
  • Different device types
  • Different user types – e.g. internal versus external

 

Additional considerations for moving to SAP Mobile Start

SAP Mobile Start is an optional component to provide a native launchpad for mobile devices on Apple (iOS) or Android. Refer to: SAP Help - SAP Mobile Start

You will need to consider mobile device types supported and any current restrictions.
You should update your mobile device strategy to cover any impacts to your operations, such as procedures for deploying SAP Mobile Start.

 

Becoming a SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA guru

You’ll find much more on our  SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA topic page

Other helpful links:

  • See all questions and answers about SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA 
  • Follow SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA for more blogs and updates 
  • Ask a Question about SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA

Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA RIG and Customer Care team.

entry.title: Re: Changes in Workflow is not reflecting in quality system
entry.content: Hi Priyanka,This is expected behavior. Workflow steps will be executed based on the version they were started with. The reason it worked in Development but not in Quality is that in Development, you might have made the latest changes to your current version. A new version might have been generated upon activation and moved to the Quality system... Typically the transport will carry only the latest changes

entry.title: Difference between custom report and fbl3n data results
entry.content:
left (ALV custom report result) right (fbl3n tcode result)

when matched there are hkont (GL/Account) & belnr (DcoumentNo) fields that do not appear in the ALV custom report, even though the query has pulled the others, what do you think is the reason? here is my code :

FORM fetch_data.

  " show data based on PERIOD or POSTING DATE
  IF p_period = 'X'. "Jika p_period is true

    SELECT bukrs belnr gjahr budat monat waers hwaer blart bldat
     INTO TABLE i_bkpf
     FROM bkpf AS a
     WHERE a~gjahr = s_fiscyr " fiscal year
         AND bukrs = '1000' " company code
         AND monat IN s_perio. " period

  ELSE. " Posting Date

    SELECT bukrs belnr gjahr budat monat waers hwaer blart bldat
      INTO TABLE i_bkpf
      FROM bkpf AS a
      WHERE a~gjahr = s_fiscyr " fiscal year
          AND bukrs = '1000' " company code
          AND budat BETWEEN s_date-low AND s_date-high. " Posting date
  ENDIF.

  IF i_bkpf IS NOT INITIAL.

          " GET DATA FROM BSEG TABLE
      SELECT hkont bukrs belnr gjahr bschl lifnr ebeln wrbtr prctr aufnr
      INTO TABLE i_bseg
      FROM bseg
      FOR ALL ENTRIES IN i_bkpf
        WHERE gjahr = i_bkpf-gjahr
          AND bukrs = i_bkpf-bukrs
          AND belnr = i_bkpf-belnr " Document Number
          AND hkont IN s_hkont.

    " GET VENDOR DATA
    IF i_bseg IS NOT INITIAL.

      SELECT lifnr name1 stras ort01 pstlz
      INTO TABLE i_lfnr
      FROM lfa1
      FOR ALL ENTRIES IN i_bseg
        WHERE lifnr = i_bseg-lifnr.
    ENDIF.

    IF i_bseg IS NOT INITIAL.

      SELECT e~ebeln e~bedat e~lifnr f~matnr f~menge f~netpr
        INTO TABLE i_ekkp
         FROM ekko AS e
         LEFT JOIN ekpo AS f
         ON e~ebeln = f~ebeln " Purchasing Document
      FOR ALL ENTRIES IN i_bseg
        WHERE lifnr = i_bseg-lifnr
        AND e~ebeln = i_bseg-ebeln.

    ENDIF.

    IF i_bseg IS NOT INITIAL.

      LOOP AT i_bkpf INTO ls_bkpf.
        LOOP AT i_bseg INTO ls_bseg WHERE bukrs = ls_bkpf-bukrs AND gjahr = ls_bkpf-gjahr AND belnr = ls_bkpf-belnr.
          CLEAR ls_result.

          IF ls_bseg-wrbtr IS NOT INITIAL.

            ls_bseg-wrbtr = ls_bseg-wrbtr * 100.

          ENDIF.

          IF ls_bseg-lifnr IS NOT INITIAL.
            READ TABLE i_lfnr INTO ls_lfnr WITH KEY lifnr = ls_bseg-lifnr BINARY SEARCH.
            MOVE-CORRESPONDING ls_lfnr TO ls_result.
          ENDIF.

          IF ls_bseg-ebeln IS NOT INITIAL.
            READ TABLE i_ekkp INTO ls_ekkp WITH KEY ebeln = ls_bseg-ebeln BINARY SEARCH.
            MOVE-CORRESPONDING ls_ekkp TO ls_result.
          ENDIF.

          IF sy-subrc EQ 0.
            MOVE-CORRESPONDING ls_bkpf TO ls_result.
            MOVE-CORRESPONDING ls_bseg TO ls_result.
            APPEND ls_result TO lt_result.
          ENDIF.

        ENDLOOP.
      ENDLOOP.
    ENDIF.

    IF p_sum_gl = 'X'.
      PERFORM display_sum_gl.
    ENDIF.

    IF p_sum_vn = 'X'.
      PERFORM display_sum_vn.
    ENDIF.

  ENDIF.

ENDFORM.                    "fetch_data

entry.title: Re: How to view and change the settlement profile for a p...
entry.content:
Hi @alexisme ,

Settlement Profile can be viewed in the following customization path, Accounting > Overhead Costs > Overhead Cost Processes > Period End closing > Create Settlement Profile

 

 

Settlement Rule assigned to a production order can be viewed from fiori app 'Display Production Order (CO03)' using the following path Menu > Header > Settlement Rule. Once you click on Settlement rule, it directs you to the new tab for Order-Event based where it displays the posting rule details. 

 

Thanks and Regards,

Prabha A

Best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Need to add custom fields in APP - Repost Line Items...
entry.content: Reposting line items in cost accounting is crucial for financial accuracy. Start by identifying any inaccuracies in cost entries. Then, adjust and repost these entries with the correct values. It's important to document the reasons for these adjustments and obtain the necessary approvals. Afterward, reconcile the accounts to ensure that financial statements reflect true costs. Maintaining an audit trail by recording both original and adjusted entries is essential for transparency. This process ensures compliance with accounting standards, enhances accuracy, and supports informed decision-making. For more financial insights, visit rosemarywater.site.

entry.title: Re: Duplicate Inspection lot creation on Sales Return Ins...
entry.content:
Hi All,

1. If we deactivates the 08 inspection type from that material, inspection lot will not be created but it will not work as client performs plant to plant stock transfer process too.

2. Even if we de-activate the quality inspection at 453 & 311 movement types, still its generating the inspection lot as it is S/4 to EWM stock transfer process at the time of Warehouse task generation.

3. However  if we de-activate the quality inspection at 453 & 311 movement types, the Inspection lot will not be generated in S/4 Hana system.

4. Only Solution i can see is the Enhancement, i.e. whenever the inbound delivery is created and once the warehouse task generates, system will check the movement types (453/311) in Table, if those movement types available there in that table, system will not generate the Inspection lots.

Thanks You

entry.title: Re: Extract attributes from XML
entry.content:
Hi Rakesh, 

There are multiple ways to achieve your requirement to extract the values as requested. 

The simplest among them would be to use a Content Modifier and extract the attribute values to be stored in properties. This can be achieved using creating properties with relevant XPath expressions. We can create three properties by configuring XPaths as below for the Content Modifier

  • For ERPNumber:

    • Name: ERPNumber
    • Type: XPath
    • Value: Use the XPath expression /SimplifiedRequest/CancelExportRequest/OrdHeader_Item/item/ERPNumber

  • For ProcurementUnit_UniqueName:

    • Name: ProcurementUnit_UniqueName
    • Type: XPath
    • Value: Use the XPath expression /SimplifiedRequest/CancelExportRequest/OrdHeader_Item/item/ProcurementUnit/UniqueName

  • For UniqueName (directly under item):

    • Name: UniqueName
    • Type: XPath
    • Value: Use the XPath expression /SimplifiedRequest/CancelExportRequest/OrdHeader_Item/item/UniqueName

 

 

Alternatively, you can use groovy script or XSLT transformation if you have some other extra steps where you want to use the extracted values instead of storing in properties for further usage.
